title: rosa canina var. thuillieri
intro: Rosa canina var. thuillieri, also known as the dwarf or shrub dog rose, is a compact and thorny shrub native to Europe, with fragrant white flowers and small, red hips. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its high concentration of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. thuillieri include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
